SMALL EF-1 TORNADO TRACKED FROM THE NNW TOWARDS THE SSE AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO SOME INDUSTRIAL BUILDINGS. TORNADO WINDS STACKED/PUSHED CARS INTO AND ON TOP OF EACH OTHER IN A PARKING LOT. A LARGE SEMI-TRAILER WAS ALSO SLID INTO SEVERAL VEHICLES. THE TORNADO CAUSED SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO A LARGE STORAGE FACILITY.

BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO MOIVED WEST TO EAST ACROSS LA PORTE AND CAUSED CONSIDERABLE TREE AND POWER LINE DAMAGE EAST OF HWY 146.

A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO MOVED ACROSS LA PORTE AND MORGANS POINT. TORNADO WAS WITNESSED TO START NEAR THE RAILROAD TRACKS/HWY 146 AND IT TRACKED ESE ACROSS THE BARBOURS CUT PORT FACILITY AND SECTIONS OF MORGANS POINT. THE MORE SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS TO INDUSTRIAL AREAS AROUND THE PORT COMPLEX. LARGE STEEL STORAGE TANKS WERE BENT INWARD FROM THE WIND... AND MANY LARGE... METAL SHIPPING CONTAINERS WERE TOPPLED OVER.
